http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/verbatimLoc.description: The original textual description of the place.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/verbatimLon.name: Verbatim Longitude
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/verbatimLon.description: The verbatim original longitude of the Location. The coordinate ellipsoid, geodeticDatum, or full Spatial Reference System (SRS) for these coordinates should be stored in verbatimSRS and the coordinate system should be stored in verbatimCoordinateSystem.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/verbatimSRS.name: Verbatim SRS
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/verbatimSRS.description: The ellipsoid, geodetic datum, or spatial reference system (SRS) upon which coordinates given in verbatimLatitude and verbatimLongitude, or verbatimCoordinates are based.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/waterBody.name: Water Body
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/waterBody.description: The name of the water body in which the Location occurs. Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names.

http://iptc.org/std/Iptc4xmpExt/2008-02-.name: World Region
http://iptc.org/std/Iptc4xmpExt/2008-02-.description: The name of a world region of a location. This element is at the first (topI) level of a topdown geographical hierarchy.

http://ns.adobe.com/xap/1.0/CreateDate.name: Original Date and Time
http://ns.adobe.com/xap/1.0/CreateDate.description: The date and time the resource was created. For a digital file, this need not match a file-system creation time. For a freshly created resource, it should be close to that time, modulo the time taken to write the file. Later file transfer, copying, and so on, can make the file-system time arbitrarily different.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/timeOfDay.name: Time of Day
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/timeOfDay.description: Free text information beyond exact clock times.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/dateIdentif.name: Date Identified
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/dateIdentif.description: The date on which the subject was determined as representing the Taxon.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/identificat.name: Identification Qualifier
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/identificat.description: A brief phrase or a standard term ("cf.", "aff.") to express the determiner's doubts about the Identification.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/identifiedB.name: Identified By
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/identifiedB.description: A list (concatenated and separated) of names of people, groups, or organizations who assigned the Taxon to the subject.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/lifeStage.name: Subject Life Stage
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/lifeStage.description: The age class or life stage of the biological individual(s) at the time the Occurrence was recorded.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/nameAccordi.name: Name According To
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/nameAccordi.description: The reference to the source in which the specific taxon concept circumscription is defined or implied - traditionally signified by the Latin "sensu" or "sec." (from secundum, meaning "according to"). For taxa that result from identifications, a reference to the keys, monographs, experts and other sources should be given.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/otherScienti.name: Other Scientific Name
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/otherScienti.description: One or several Scientific Taxon Names that are synonyms to the Scientific Taxon Name may be provided here.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/preparation.name: Subject Preparation Technique
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/preparation.description: A list (concatenated and separated) of preparations and preservation methods for a specimen.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/scientificN.name: Scientific Taxon Name
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/scientificN.description: The full scientific name, with authorship and date information if known. When forming part of an Identification, this should be the name in lowest level taxonomic rank that can be determined. This term should not contain identification qualifications, which should instead be supplied in the IdentificationQualifier term.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/scientificN.name: Scientific Name ID
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/scientificN.description: An identifier for the nomenclatural (not taxonomic) details of a scientific name.

http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/sex.name: Subject Sex
http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/sex.description: The sex of the biological individual(s) represented in the Occurrence.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectOrien.name: Subject Orientation
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectOrien.description: Specific orientation (= direction, view angle) of the subject represented in the media resource with respect to the acquisition device, denoted by an IRI.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectOrien.name: Subject Orientation (literal)
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectOrien.description: Specific orientation (= direction, view angle) of the subject represented in the media resource with respect to the acquisition device, denoted by a controlled value string.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectPart.name: Subject Part
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectPart.description: The portion or product of organism morphology, behaviour, environment, etc. that is either predominantly shown or particularly well exemplified by the media resource, denoted by an IRI.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectPartL.name: Subject Part (literal)
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/subjectPartL.description: The portion or product of organism morphology, behaviour, environment, etc. that is either predominantly shown or particularly well exemplified by the media resource, denoted by a controlled value string.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/taxonCount.name: Taxon Count
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/taxonCount.description: An exact or estimated number of taxa at the lowest applicable taxon rank (usually species or infraspecific) represented by the media resource (item or collection).

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/taxonCoverag.name: Taxon Coverage
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/taxonCoverag.description: A higher taxon (e. g., a genus, family, or order) at the level of the genus or higher, that covers all taxa that are the primary subject of the resource (which may be a media item or a collection).

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/captureDevic.name: Capture Device
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/captureDevic.description: Free form text describing the device or devices used to create the resource.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/digitization.name: Date and Time Digitized
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/digitization.description: Date the first digital version was created, if different from Original Date and Time found in the Temporal Coverage Vocabulary.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/frameRate.name: Frame Rate
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/frameRate.description: The decimal fraction representing the frequency (rate) at which consecutive images (frames) were captured in real time for a moving image, expressed as the number of frames per second.

http://iptc.org/std/Iptc4xmpExt/2008-02-.name: Location Created
http://iptc.org/std/Iptc4xmpExt/2008-02-.description: The location the content of the item was created

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/resourceCrea.name: Resource Creation Technique
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/resourceCrea.description: Information about technical aspects of the creation and digitization process of the resource. This includes modification steps ("retouching") after the initial resource capture.

http://purl.org/ontology/mo/sample_rate.name: Sample Rate
http://purl.org/ontology/mo/sample_rate.description: Associates a digital signal to its sample rate.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/associatedOb.name: Associated Observation Reference
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/associatedOb.description: A reference to an observation associated with this resource.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/associatedSp.name: Associated Specimen Reference
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/associatedSp.description: A reference to a specimen associated with this resource.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/derivedFrom.name: Derived From
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/derivedFrom.description: A reference to an original resource from which the current one is derived.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/IDofContaini.name: ID of Containing Collection
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/IDofContaini.description: If the resource is contained in a Collection, this field identifies that Collection uniquely.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/providerID.name: Provider ID
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/providerID.description: A globally unique ID of the provider of the current AC metadata record.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/relatedResou.name: Related Resource ID
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/relatedResou.description: Resource related in ways not specified through a collection, e.g., before-after images; time-lapse series; different orientations/angles of view

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/accessURI.name: Access URI
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/accessURI.description: A URI that uniquely identifies a service that provides a representation of the underlying resource.

http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/format.name: Format (literal)
http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/format.description: The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.

http://purl.org/dc/terms/format.name: Format (IRI)
http://purl.org/dc/terms/format.description: The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/furtherInfor.name: Further Information URL
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/furtherInfor.description: The URL of a Web site that provides additional information about the version of the media resource that is provided by the Service Access Point.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/hashFunction.name: Hash Function
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/hashFunction.description: The cryptographic hash function used to compute the value given in the Hash Value.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/hashValue.name: Hash
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/hashValue.description: The value computed by a hash function applied to the media that will be delivered at the access point.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/licensingExc.name: Licensing Exception Statement
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/licensingExc.description: The licensing statement for this variant of the media resource if different from that given in the License Statement property of the resource.

http://ns.adobe.com/exif/1.0/PixelXDimen.name: Image Width
http://ns.adobe.com/exif/1.0/PixelXDimen.description: Information specific to compressed data. When a compressed file is recorded, the valid width of the meaningful image shall be recorded in this tag, whether or not there is padding data or a restart marker. This tag shall not exist in an uncompressed file.

http://ns.adobe.com/exif/1.0/PixelYDimen.name: Image Height
http://ns.adobe.com/exif/1.0/PixelYDimen.description: Information specific to compressed data. When a compressed file is recorded, the valid height of the meaningful image shall be recorded in this tag, whether or not there is padding data or a restart marker. This tag shall not exist in an uncompressed file.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/ServiceAcces.name: Service Access Point Class
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/ServiceAcces.description: A specific digital representation of a media resource.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/serviceExpec.name: Service Expectation
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/serviceExpec.description: A term that describes what service expectations users may have of the ac:accessURI.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/variant.name: Variant (IRI)
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/variant.description: The category describing this Service Access Point variant, denoted by an IRI.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/variantDescr.name: Variant Description
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/variantDescr.description: Text that describes this Service Access Point variant

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/variantLiter.name: Variant (literal)
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/variantLiter.description: The category describing this Service Access Point variant, denoted by a controlled value string.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/endTime.name: End Time in Seconds
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/endTime.description: The end of a temporal region, specified as an absolute offset relative to the beginning of the media item (this corresponds to Normal Play Time RFC 2326), specified as seconds, with an optional fractional part to indicate milliseconds or finer.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/endTimestamp.name: End Timestamp
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/endTimestamp.description: The end of a temporal region, specified as real-world clock time ISO 8601 timestamps, using UTC timezone, with an optional fractional part to indicate milliseconds or finer. There is no limit on the number of decimal places for the decimal fraction.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/hasROI.name: Has Region of Interest
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/hasROI.description: A region of interest located within the subject media item.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/heightFrac.name: Fractional Height
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/heightFrac.description: The height of the bounding rectangle, expressed as a decimal fraction of the height of the media item.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/isROIOf.name: Is Region of Interest of
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/isROIOf.description: The media item within which a region of interest is located.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/mediaDuratio.name: Media Duration
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/mediaDuratio.description: The playback duration of an audio or video file in seconds.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/mediaSpeed.name: Media Speed
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/mediaSpeed.description: The decimal fraction representing the natural speed over the encoded speed.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/radius.name: Radius
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/radius.description: The radius of a bounding circle or arc, expressed as a fraction of the width of the media item.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/RegionOfInte.name: Region of Interest Class
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/RegionOfInte.description: A designated region of a media item bounded in dimensions appropriate for the media type.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/startTime.name: Start Time in Seconds
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/startTime.description: The beginning of a temporal region, specified as an absolute offset relative to the beginning of the media item (this corresponds to Normal Play Time RFC 2326), specified as seconds, with an optional fractional part to indicate milliseconds or finer.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/startTimesta.name: Start Timestamp
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/startTimesta.description: The beginning of a temporal region, specified as real-world clock time ISO 8601 timestamps, using UTC timezone, with an optional fractional part to indicate milliseconds or finer. There is no limit on the number of decimal places for the decimal fraction.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/widthFrac.name: Fractional Width
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/widthFrac.description: The width of the bounding rectangle, expressed as a decimal fraction of the width of the media item.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/xFrac.name: Fractional X
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/xFrac.description: The horizontal position of a reference point, measured from the left side of the media item and expressed as a decimal fraction of the width of the media item.

http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/yFrac.name: Fractional Y
http://rs.tdwg.org/ac/terms/yFrac.description: The vertical position of a reference point, measured from the top of the media item and expressed as a decimal fraction of the height of the media item.
